source-discipline.reason: v10.15 queue entry withdrawn per owner directive (no PI bump unless critical armature). Withdrawal kept as a 'considered and rejected' note with explicit citation of architecture v2.3 <migration_and_growth> ('state belongs in DRs, audit scripts, or the DB, not in PI'). Audit trail preserved without re-queuing the patch.

bias_direction: This edit reverses an action from the prior commit (be146a2) by the same session. The bias risk is the appearance of churn — owner correction triggered a same-session walkback. The 'considered and rejected' framing is honest about the sequence; the alternative (silent removal) would lose the audit signal of why this isn't queued.
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: An outside reviewer would observe that a session that adds and removes a PI queue entry within ~30 minutes is symptomatic of insufficient up-front deliberation. Counter: the queue addition was a defensible mirror of a same-session DR; the removal followed an explicit owner directive that arrived after the queue was visible to the owner. The two-step is the intended human-in-the-loop pattern for PI changes; the audit trail of both steps is the feature, not a defect.
